title: Defensive Retrieval Research — future study on retrieval-augmented-generation poisoning

fields.vision: A future empirical research program extending the qualitative capstone into RAG corruption conditions.
fields.mechanism: It crosses named poison types with prompt-discipline or review conditions, inherits outcome measures from the capstone, and can add timing, saturation, authority-wrapper, omission, and analyst-burden metrics. It can test whether slow poisoning, fast poisoning, and decoy contamination create different verification costs, similar to how mixed-speed drone and missile attacks stress layered air-defense systems.
fields.built_components: This page is presented as a research idea rather than as completed work because that is what it is. There is no code yet, no pilot run, no IRB conversation; the two-axis design above is the only work product at this stage. Surfacing the page at this state is the point — the honesty about where it sits matters more than dressing it up to look further along, and an external reviewer would want to see it at this state rather than after it has been quietly tightened. The foundation to make the study runnable already exists. What is missing is a collaborative design pass — selecting the canonical retrieval-poison taxonomy, locking the target sample size against a power calculation, and choosing the publication venue and the IRB pathway. Those are the decisions that need to be made before the technical foundation work begins.
fields.source_utilization: Source use is organized by role: method authorities, domain evidence, technical background, implementation evidence, and claim limits. The page should show why each source matters; direct quotations and stronger factual claims still require exact passage anchors before publication.
fields.plan: Three decision steps gate the technical work. The order matters because doing foundation work before the design decisions are settled would lock in a study the design pass might want to change. Step 01 retrieval-poison taxonomy scan Literature scan to determine which IC-AI-credible taxonomies the five named poison types map onto — and whether the study extends a published taxonomy or proposes its own. Step 02 Five-by-four design lock Target sample size derived from a power calculation against the capstone's effect sizes. The design is on the page; the parameters are not yet settled. Step 03 Venue and IRB pathway Whether the capstone IRB precedent extends or a new submission is required; which journal the design is targeted toward. Step 04 Poison-harness foundation Built in the research-library corpus plane (Row 06) — once the design is locked, this is what makes the experiment runnable at corpus scale. Step 05 Matched-pair coding execution Run under the locked design; scored against the deterministic instruments. The coding methodology is inherited from the capstone — execution rather than design.
fields.status: Status: This page is presented as a research idea rather than as completed work because that is what it is. There is no code yet, no pilot run, no IRB conversation; the two-axis design above is the only work product at this stage. Surfacing the page at this state is the point — the honesty about where it sits matters more than dressing it up to look further along, and an external reviewer would want to see it at this state rather than after it has been quietly tightened.
fields.remaining_gaps: Remaining gaps: three decision steps gate the technical work. The order matters because doing foundation work before the design decisions are settled would lock in a study the design pass might want to change.
fields.detailed_source_vision: A publication-quality empirical study extending the capstone work. The capstone established that analysts working under structured-analytic-technique discipline can catch AI-introduced errors when the corrupted evidence is presented to them; the natural next question is which kinds of corruption are hardest to catch under which prompt-discipline conditions. The study is structured as a two-axis matrix — five named retrieval poison types crossed with four named prompt-discipline conditions, twenty experimental cells in total — and inherits its outcome measures from the capstone's matched-pair design, so the new study is methodologically continuous with the work the capstone has already validated. The foundation to make this study runnable already exists across the portfolio; what is missing is the collaborative design pass and the IRB pathway.
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: The design is a two-axis matrix — twenty cells, one per experimental condition. Five named corruption types form the rows; four named prompt-discipline conditions form the columns.

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.

public_pitch.what_it_is: A future empirical research program extending the qualitative capstone into RAG corruption conditions.
public_pitch.distinctive_mechanism: It crosses named poison types with prompt-discipline or review conditions, inherits outcome measures from the capstone, and can add timing, saturation, authority-wrapper, omission, and analyst-burden metrics. It can test whether slow poisoning, fast poisoning, and decoy contamination create different verification costs, similar to how mixed-speed drone and missile attacks stress layered air-defense systems.
public_pitch.why_it_matters: It makes the next study more than a generic Poison-RAG test: the real question is which adversarial evidence pressures impose the greatest verification burden, where the human reviewer or retrieval defense spends scarce attention, and when a defensive layer becomes easier to saturate than to defeat directly.
public_pitch.pitch_integrity_note: Mechanism-first public pitch inserted after the Nexus Lab mechanism-erasure audit on 2026-05-13; source basis: rendered correction table.

pitch_integrity_audit.standard: object, distinctive mechanism, value, built state, and non-overclaim discipline must be visible.

instruments.input: clean documents, poisoned variants, source identity, poison specification, and admission rule
instruments.operation: tests whether contaminated material enters the corpus before indexing and records the attack surface at the corpus boundary
instruments.output: admission decision and attack-surface record as a reviewable artifact
instruments.upstream_dependency: Requires clean documents, poisoned variants, source identity, poison specification, and admission rule
instruments.downstream_use: Feeds admission decision and attack-surface record as a reviewable artifact; later claims should not rely on this step until its expected output is present.
instruments.proof: Expected proof artifact: admission decision and attack-surface record as a reviewable artifact
instruments.status: Status: This page is presented as a research idea rather than as completed work because that is what it is. There is no code yet, no pilot run, no IRB.
instruments.gap: Verify one representative case: the step should accept clean documents, poisoned variants, source identity, poison specification, and admission rule and produce admission decision and attack-surface record as a.
instruments.verification_check: Replay one clean/poisoned case through Clean / Poisoned Evidence Admission; confirm exposure, uptake or rejection, reviewer burden, and output: admission decision and attack-surface record....
